title: Roku Q1 2023 Earnings - Deadline
text: Roku delivered first-quarter results with mixed performance. The streaming platform achieved revenue of $741 million, representing just 1% growth year-over-year, but exceeded Wall Street expectations which had projected $708.5 million. The company reported per-share losses of $1.38, matching analyst forecasts of $1.37. This represented a significant widening from losses of 19 cents during the same quarter in 2022. Roku expanded its user base to 71.6 million active accounts across connected devices and smart TVs, achieving 17% year-over-year growth. However, average revenue per user declined 5% to $40.67. Users streamed a record 25.1 billion hours during the quarter, equating to approximately 3.9 hours per active account daily. The company acknowledged ongoing difficulties in its advertising segment. In its shareholder letter, Roku noted that "inflation and worries about a looming recession will constrain the business" in the coming quarter. Travel and health-wellness categories showed potential improvement, while financial services and media-entertainment sectors faced continued pressure. Roku projects Q2 losses will continue but expects sequential revenue improvement to $770 million.
